title: Season Podium Finishers
description: You are given three tables tracking players, seasons, and their rankings in each season. A season is either 'ranked' or 'casual'. Return usernames of players who finished in the top 3 (rank <= 3) in at least 2 different ranked seasons. Order alphabetically.
inputFormat:
players(id, username, country)
seasons(id, name, game_mode)
season_scores(id, player_id, season_id, points, rank)
outputFormat: One username per line, ordered alphabetically.
constraints: Only count seasons where game_mode = 'ranked'. Use JOIN, GROUP BY, and HAVING COUNT >= 2. Include ORDER BY username. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT p.username FROM players p JOIN season_scores ss ON p.id = ss.player_id JOIN seasons s ON ss.season_id = s.id WHERE s.game_mode = 'ranked' AND ss.rank <= 3 GROUP BY p.id, p.username HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY p.username;
hint: Join all three tables, filter by game_mode and rank, then use GROUP BY + HAVING COUNT.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE players (id INTEGER, username TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE seasons (id INTEGER, name TEXT, game_mode TEXT); CREATE TABLE season_scores (id INTEGER, player_id INTEGER, season_id INTEGER, points INTEGER, rank INTEGER); INSERT INTO players VALUES (1,'Alice','US'),(2,'Bob','UK'),(3,'Charlie','DE'),(4,'Diana','FR'); INSERT INTO seasons VALUES (1,'Spring','ranked'),(2,'Summer','casual'),(3,'Autumn','ranked'),(4,'Winter','ranked'); INSERT INTO season_scores VALUES (1,1,1,500,1),(2,1,2,400,2),(3,1,3,600,3),(4,2,1,300,5),(5,2,3,450,4),(6,3,1,550,2),(7,3,4,480,1),(8,4,2,350,1),(9,4,3,400,5);
